commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Fix Capitalization column format mismatch and duplicate difference column

Rewrote CapTable to use a data-driven COLUMNS array with explicit formatters
per column, table-layout: fixed with <colgroup> widths, and a single loop

with conditional formatting is now the only difference column. All cells
are guaranteed to match their header by construction.

commit.message:
opus-2026-03-07-S45: per(W) analysis, cover polynomial research summary

Key findings:
- per(W(1)) = D_n (subfactorial) universally for all tournaments
- per(W(x)) palindromic for tournaments with no transitive arc pattern
- odd_per(W(x)) encodes directed odd-cycle covers weighted by forward edges
- Cover polynomial C(T;x,y) cannot give F(T,x) — different variables
- F(T,x) lives in Redei-Berge framework, not cover polynomial framework

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace shortcode type/language maps with fallback-based functions

Instead of maintaining _SHORTCODE_TYPE_MAP and _LANGUAGE_MAP that must
be extended for every new file type, derive the shortcode type and
language from the extension. Unknown extensions fall back to "code"
with a stderr warning, making the system open for extension.

commit.message:
Add unit tests for fuzzy-search.el

Create comprehensive test suite tests/fuzzy-search-tests.el with 40+ test cases covering:
- fuzzy-search-token class: creation and comparison
- fuzzy-search-token-differences class: creation, first/last accessors
- fuzzy-search-min-difference: comparison logic
- fuzzy-search--pre-less-token: backward search for less tokens
- fuzzy-search-compare-tokens: token list comparison
- fuzzy-search--clear-highlights: overlay cleanup
- fuzzy-search--highlight-token: highlighting for all diff-types
- fuzzy-search--region-tokens: token extraction from buffer regions
- fuzzy-search--query-tokens: query string tokenization
- fuzzy-search-token--builder: token creation utility
- Integration tests for fuzzy-search function

commit.message:
Bump version to 0.1.82

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add deviceId support for stream resumption

When a device creates a stream with a deviceId, the backend checks
if there's already an existing (active or stopped) stream from that
device. If found, it reuses the same stream ID instead of creating
a duplicate. This lets a camera restart its stream into the same
card on the website.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add AV1 RTL encoder: Verilog pipeline, C++ testbench, bitstream writer

Full AV1 hardware encoder with 8x8 block pipeline:
- RTL modules: fetch, intra prediction, transform, quantize, inverse path, motion estimation, entropy, bitstream, reconstruction
- C++ testbench with Verilator: captures quantized coefficients from RTL, produces AV1/IVF bitstream
- AV1 bitstream writer with CDF-based range coding, coefficient coding (Phase 2), partition tree
- Support scripts, Docker config, Makefile build system
- 1280x720 keyframe encodes successfully, coefficient coding in progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.7.0 — Pivot to mid-range non-reasoning model lineup

Replace flagship reasoning models (GPT-5.2, Gemini 2.5 Pro, Grok 3) with
non-reasoning counterparts (GPT-5 mini, Gemini 2.5 Flash, Grok 3 mini).
Reasoning models consumed max_tokens budget with thinking tokens, causing
widespread truncation. Add reasoning_effort support to gen_completions.py.
Raise max_tokens to 16384. Archive 2,635 existing completions (~$40 sunk cost).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix row sizing race condition and add column stretch/grow (DASH-8)

Row sizing (1x/2x/3x) failed because handleRowOptionSelect made two
separate setState calls that raced — the second overwrote the first.
Combined both mutations into a single handleChangeRowSizing handler
that applies changeRowMode + changeRowHeight on one DashboardModel
instance, producing one setState call.

Also adds column sizing support (G, S, 1/4, 1/3, 1/2, 2/3) with
colModes on DashboardModel, popover UI in LayoutGridContainer, and
CSS grid template generation.

commit.message:
Mobile app features, search, invoices, filters & QA checklist (#134)

* Add mobile app features and comprehensive QA checklist update

New mobile screens: search, create invoice, enhanced invoice detail (send/mark paid),
task list filters (status/priority/sort) with swipe-to-complete, project detail with
4-tab layout (overview/tasks/time/budget). Fix Google OAuth redirect URLs, update
splash screen text color. QA checklist expanded with ~180 new mobile test items
covering all screens, haptics, navigation, and edge cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Mobile App (iOS) section to feature catalog

New category 13 with 62 features covering auth, navigation, dashboard
(activity rings, insights, speed dial), tasks (filters, swipe-to-complete),
project detail (4-tab layout), invoices (create/send/mark paid), search,
and UX polish (haptics, animations, pull-to-refresh). Total features
now ~268 across 13 categories.

Add video upload analysis, camera pause/resume, and gitignore updates

- Vision page: drag-and-drop video upload with YOLO frame analysis and behavior timeline
- Vision page: Pause/Resume button for live camera feed
- Backend: POST /analyze_video using COCO yolov8l for reliable cow detection with aspect-ratio behavior classification
- Backend: /pause_camera, /resume_camera, /camera_status endpoints
- .gitignore: exclude model weights (*.pt), YOLO runs, archive data, and venv

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add Bull — GitHub issue triage daemon (#8)

* feat: add BullConfig struct with validation and defaults

Add BullConfig, BullCommentsConfig, and BullLabelsConfig structs to
the config package for the Bull GitHub issue triage daemon. Includes
YAML parsing, defaults (poll_interval_sec=60, triage_mode=standard,
bull: prefix labels), env var resolution for github_token, and
validation (token required when enabled, triage_mode must be
standard or full). Adds 4 testdata fixtures and 11 tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add BullIssue model and SourceIssue field on Car

Add BullIssue GORM model (internal/models/bull_issue.go) with fields:
ID, IssueNumber (unique index), CarID, LastKnownStatus, TriageSummary,
TriageMode, LastSyncedAt, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt. Add SourceIssue int
field to Car model for linking cars back to GitHub issues. Register
BullIssue in AllModels() for AutoMigrate. Includes field tag tests,
instantiation tests, and CRUD tests with unique constraint verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add GitHub API client wrapper with rate limiting

Add internal/bull/github.go wrapping google/go-github v68 with:
- NewClient constructor with oauth2 token auth
- ListNewIssues, GetIssue, AddLabel, RemoveLabel, AddComment,
  CloseIssue, ListReleases methods
- Rate limit awareness: automatic backoff when remaining < threshold
- 403 rate limit retry with sleep-until-reset

12 tests using httptest mock server, no real API calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add AI triage prompt builder and parser

Add internal/bull/prompt.go with:
- BuildTriagePrompt: generates AI prompts for issue triage in
  standard mode (classify, prioritize, assign track) and full mode
  (adds design_notes and dependency suggestions)
- ParseTriageResult: extracts JSON from AI responses including
  markdown code fence unwrapping, validates required fields
- TriageResult and IssueContext structs

12 tests covering both modes, code context inclusion/omission,
JSON parsing, code fence extraction, and validation errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add label management with idempotent operations

Add internal/bull/labels.go with LabelClient interface and functions:
EnsureLabels (creates missing bull labels on repo), ApplyLabel,
RemoveBullLabel (swallows 404), RemoveAllBullLabels. Add CreateLabel
and ListLabels methods to GitHubClient. All operations are idempotent.
8 tests with mock client.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add heuristic triage filter for zero-cost pre-screening

Add internal/bull/filter.go with FilterIssue function that rejects
issues before AI triage: nil issues, pull requests, ignored labels,
short/empty bodies, already-tracked issues, and near-duplicate titles.
Fix flaky rate limit backoff test timing. 11 filter tests + test fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add reverse sync from car status to GitHub issues

Add internal/bull/sync.go with SyncCarStatuses function that detects
car status changes and applies corresponding GitHub label transitions
and optional comments. Supports: open/ready/claimed → InProgress,
merged → FixMerged + comment, cancelled → remove all labels + comment.
Idempotent and crash-safe. 10 tests with mock client/store.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add AI triage execution pipeline

Add internal/bull/execute.go with ExecuteTriage function implementing
the full triage pipeline: heuristic filter → AI prompt → parse result
→ act on classification (create car for bug/task, answer questions,
reject with configurable template). Uses interfaces for testability.
Fix flaky 403 rate limit test timing. 10 triage tests + test fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add release detection and issue closing for bull sync

SyncReleases polls GitHub releases API, closes issues with fix-merged
status, and persists last-checked timestamp for idempotent re-runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add bull daemon loop with six-phase polling cycle

RunDaemon orchestrates: GitHub poll, filter, heuristic, AI triage,
reverse sync, and release scan. Supports graceful shutdown, rate
limit backoff, and error resilience across phases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add bull entry point and integration test suite

StartOpts/Start() validates config, token, and tracks before
delegating to RunDaemon. Integration tests verify all 10 bull
scenarios: triage, sync, release detection, filtering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add ry bull and ry bull triage CLI commands

ry bull starts the daemon with signal handling and graceful shutdown.
ry bull triage <number> runs one-shot triage on a single GitHub issue.
Both share --config/-c flag for config loading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Bull Helm chart deployment and documentation

- Create bull-deployment.yaml mirroring telegraph pattern (enabled guard,
  GITHUB_TOKEN from auth secret, config mount, no git-clone initContainer)
- Add bull section to values.yaml (enabled: false, pollIntervalSec,
  triageMode, comments, labels)
- Add bull config block to configmap.yaml (conditional on bull.enabled)
- Update CI test-values (full and kind) with bull examples
- Create docs/bull-setup.md (overview, prereqs, config ref, label scheme,
  triage modes, K8s notes, troubleshooting)
- Update README.md with Bull terminology and usage section
- Update ARCHITECTURE.md with Bull daemon/triage/label lifecycle sections
- Add commented-out bull section to railyard.example.yaml

All helm lint and template checks pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve golangci-lint issues in bull package

Fix gofmt formatting, remove unused struct fields, simplify embedded
field selectors, and remove unused variable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve remaining gofmt formatting issues

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: wire bull daemon into orchestration startup

- Add BullSession helper and auto-start bull tmux session when
  bull.enabled is true in config (same pattern as Telegraph)
- Implement Store backed by gorm.DB satisfying DaemonStore interface
- Add BullMeta model for persisting last_release_check timestamp
- Wire Start() to construct real dependencies and call RunDaemon
- Add nil DB validation to Start()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: parse GitHub owner/repo from repo URL in bull

The config repo field is a full URL (e.g. https://github.com/org/repo.git)
but the GitHub API client needs separate owner and repo strings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: wire AI provider into bull triage pipeline

- Add agent_provider field to BullConfig (defaults to global agent_provider)
- Create ProviderAI adapter that implements TriageAI using
  engine.AgentProvider.BuildPromptCommand for one-shot prompt execution
- Wire AI into DaemonOpts in Start() so triage actually runs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: advance poll cursor before fetching to avoid blind spot

Capture the poll boundary timestamp before calling ListNewIssues so
issues updated during processing are included in the next cycle.
Previously lastPoll was set at cycle end, which could permanently
skip issues updated between the API call and the timestamp assignment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: paginate ListNewIssues to avoid dropping issues beyond page 1

Loop on resp.NextPage and aggregate all pages, matching the pattern
already used in ListLabels. Previously only the first 100 issues were
returned, which could silently drop work on initial startup or in
busy repos.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip duplicate title check when tracked title is empty

Empty titles in ExistingIssue caused strings.Contains(title, "") to
match every incoming issue, rejecting all new issues as duplicates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: correct seesaw button remapping and add plugin hook logger (#27)

## Summary
- Fix seesaw button index remapping: physical buttons are reversed
relative to display zones due to 90° rotation, so apply `(3 - index)`
before handedness mapping
- Restore button labels after display clear (previously labels were lost
after responding to a notification)
- Add `logger.js` module to plugin hook scripts for send/recv message
visibility and improved debugging
- Add mise tasks (`run`, `build`, `test-notification`) and
`scripts/test-notifications.js` helper

## Test plan
- [x] Press each physical button and verify the correct display zone
highlights
- [x] Send a notification, respond, confirm labels are restored on the
display
- [x] Check plugin hook log output for send/recv messages during
notification flow
- [ ] Run `mise run test-notification` to verify the test script works

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Pika 探针集成 + 双探针架构

Backend:

commit.message:
docs: update businessreview with E2E round 3 results

6x improvement in messages-to-planning (12+ → 2 messages).
Full autonomous flow: describe → 2 exchanges → build phase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Buddhi Phase Machine — ORIENT/EXECUTE/VERIFY/COMPLETE (303 tests)

Replace hardcoded round_num >= 3 with intelligent phase derivation
from Chitta's impressions. Phase is self-awareness — Chitta knows
"where am I?" from "what have I done?"

Phases:
  ORIENT   — exploring/reading (< 2 reads)
  EXECUTE  — making changes (2+ reads or recent writes)
  VERIFY   — wrote files, no recent writes (time to test)
  COMPLETE — writes + successful bash (tests passed)

Phase transitions:
  - EXECUTE→VERIFY: injects "consider running tests" guidance
  - Error regression (2+ recent errors): back to ORIENT
  - Phase visible in BuddhiDirective.phase and AgentUsage.buddhi_phase

14 new tests for phase machine and guidance injection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
